# API Reference

This page describes the APIs for ConnectKit which you can reference during development.

## Components and Hooks

Below is a list of the React.js components and Hooks provided as part of the ConnectKit library:

| Component / Hook | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| `<ConnectKitProvider />` | Provides state and data to various ConnectKit components. Wrap it around your React.js app. |
| `<ConnectKitButton />` | The ConnectKit button. Place this component where you'd like the Connect Wallet button to appear. |
| `<ConnectKitButton.Custom />` | Design your own Connect button. Various wallet connection states are provided through render props. |
| `<Avatar />` | Change the avatar used in ConnectKit to more closely match the look and feel of your app. |
| `<ChainIcon />` | Access ConnectKit's chain icons from within your app for consistency. |
| `useModal()` | Programmatically control some aspects of the modal. |
| `useSIWE()` | Programmatically control some aspects of SIWE. |
| `useChains()` | Access the configured chains via this convenient Hook. |

## ConnectKitProvider

Wrap `<ConnectKitProvider>` around your React.js app to provide state and data to various ConnectKit components.

### theme

Sets which theme ConnectKit should use.

| Theme Prop | Description | Light/Dark Mode Support |
|------------|-------------|-------------------------|
| `default` | Default theme | Yes |
| `web95` | Web95 theme | No |
| `retro` | Retro theme | No |
| `soft` | Soft theme | No |
| `midnight` | Midnight theme | No |
| `minimal` | Minimal theme | No |
| `rounded` | Rounded theme | No |
| `nouns` | Nouns theme | No |

### mode

Sets which dark/light mode configuration ConnectKit should use.

| Mode Prop | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `auto` | Either dark or light that matches the user's system color scheme |
| `dark` | Dark mode |
| `light` | Light mode |

### customTheme

Configure individual styles for ConnectKit, overriding styles from the selected theme.

> Overriding theme variables isn't a stable feature yet and could change in future versions of ConnectKit. Only customize the theme if you're confident you can migrate any changes when upgrading.

Grouped variable sets available: Connect Wallet Button variables, Primary Button variables, Secondary Button variables, Tertiary Button variables, Modal variables, Text variables, and Miscellaneous variables.

### options

Configuration options you can toggle on/off:

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `avoidLayoutShift` | boolean | `true` | Avoids layout shift when the modal is open by adding padding to the `<body>`. Set to `false` to use your own solution with `--ck-scrollbar-width`. |
| `bufferPolyfill` | boolean | `true` | Implements a Buffer polyfill for WalletConnect by default; set to `false` to disable. |
| `customAvatar` | React.ReactNode | | Change the avatar used in ConnectKit. |
| `disableSiweRedirect` | boolean | | Disable the redirect to the SIWE page when the user connects a wallet. |
| `disclaimer` | ReactNode \| string | `null` | Disclaimer (e.g. terms and conditions) shown in the modal when not yet connected. |
| `embedGoogleFonts` | boolean | `false` | Automatically embeds the Google Font of the current theme. |
| `enforceSupportedChains` | boolean | `false` | Only allow connecting to chains supported by your app. Set `false` to allow any chain. |
| `ethereumOnboardingUrl` | string | `https://ethereum.org/en/wallets/` | Link to a resource for learning about Ethereum. |
| `hideBalance` | boolean | | Hide balance in the profile view. |
| `hideNoWalletCTA` | boolean | | Hide the "I don't have a wallet" CTA. |
| `hideQuestionMarkCTA` | boolean | | Hide the question mark that leads to the "About Wallets" page. |
| `hideRecentBadge` | boolean | `false` | Hide the "Recent" badge on the most recently connected connector. |
| `hideTooltips` | boolean | | Hide any tooltips that would normally appear. |
| `initialChainId` | number | first chain in wagmi config | Target a specific chain. Set to `0` to remove chain targeting. |
| `language` | enum | | Language: en-US, ar-AE, ca-AD, ee-EE, es-ES, fa-IR, fr-FR, pt-BR, ru-RU, tr-TR, vi-VN, zh-CN. |
| `overlayBlur` | number | | Degree of blur applied to the overlay. Alternatively use `--ck-overlay-backdrop-filter`. |
| `truncateLongENSAddress` | boolean | `true` | Truncates long ENS addresses in the connect button with ellipsis. |
| `walletConnectCTA` | 'modal' \| 'link' \| 'both' | `'link'` | Controls the WalletConnect QR button. |
| `walletConnectName` | string | `'Other Wallets'` | Change the display name of the WalletConnect option. |
| `walletOnboardingUrl` | string | `https://ethereum.org/en/wallets/find-wallet/` | Link to a resource for learning about Wallets. |

### onConnect

Callback function called when a wallet is connected. Called with the following arguments:

| Argument | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `address` | string | The connected wallet address. |
| `connectorId` | string | The ID of the wallet connector used. |

### onDisconnect

Callback function called when a wallet is disconnected.

## ConnectKitButton

Place this component where you'd like the Connect Wallet button to appear.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | string | `"Connect Wallet"` | Customize the connect button text/label. |
| `showBalance` | boolean | `false` | Display the connected wallet's balance in the connect button. |
| `showAvatar` | boolean | `true` | Display the avatar visual in the connect button when connected. |

## ConnectKitButton.Custom

Design your own Connect button. Various wallet connection states are provided through render props.

### Render Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `isConnected` | boolean | Whether or not a wallet is connected. |
| `isConnecting` | boolean | If ConnectKit is currently connecting. |
| `show` | function | Function to show the modal (bind to `onClick`). |
| `hide` | function | Function to hide the modal programmatically. |
| `address` | string | The address of the connected wallet. |
| `truncatedAddress` | string | A truncated version of the connected wallet address. |
| `ensName` | string | ENS name if available, otherwise empty string. |
| `chain` | Chain | The chain the connected wallet is connected to. |

## Hooks

### useModal

A convenience Hook to show/dismiss the modal. Must be used within a `<ConnectKitProvider>`.

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `open` | boolean | Whether or not the modal is open. |
| `setOpen` | (boolean) => void | Programmatically show/dismiss the modal. |
| `openAbout` | () => void | Open the modal on the About page. |
| `openOnboarding` | () => void | Open the modal on the Onboarding page. |
| `openProfile` | () => void | Open the modal on the Profile page. |
| `openSwitchNetworks` | () => void | Open the modal on the Switch Network prompt. |
| `openSIWE` | (triggerSIWE?: boolean) => void | Open the modal on the SIWE page. If `triggerSIWE` is `true`, the signing request is also prompted. |
| `onConnect` | (address, connectorId) => void | Callback called when a wallet is connected. |
| `onDisconnect` | () => void | Callback called when a wallet is disconnected. |

### useSIWE

A convenience Hook to access SIWE state. Must be used with Sign In With Ethereum.

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `isSignedIn` | boolean | Whether or not the wallet has signed in. |
| `data` | SIWESession | Returned data when a wallet has signed in (`address`, `chainId`). |
| `error` | Error | Error thrown if there was an error signing the message. |
| `isRejected` | boolean | If the user rejected the signing message. |
| `isError` | boolean | If there was an error signing the message. |
| `isLoading` | boolean | If the app is waiting for the user to sign the message. |
| `isSuccess` | boolean | If the user has successfully signed in. |
| `isReady` | boolean | If the app is ready for the user to sign in. |
| `signIn` | () => Promise<boolean> | Prompt the user to sign in. |
| `signOut` | () => Promise<boolean> | Sign out the user. |
| `reset` | () => void | Reset the hook to a fresh state. |

### useChains

Access your dApp's configured chains. Works well with the `ChainIcon` component.

```tsx
import { useChains, ChainIcon } from "connectkit";

const MyComponent = () => {
  const chains = useChains();
  return (
    <>
      <p>This dApp is supported on the following chains:</p>
      {chains.map((chain) => (
        <div key={chain.id}>
          <ChainIcon id={chain.id} />
          <span>{chain.name}</span>
        </div>
      ))}
    </>
  );
};
```

## Additional Components

### Avatar

Access the ConnectKit `<Avatar>` component from within your app to display the user's ENS avatar outside ConnectKit (e.g. a profile header). If you've set up a custom avatar, this component returns your custom version instead.

```tsx
import { Avatar } from "connectkit";

<Avatar name="vitalik.eth" />
<Avatar name="vitalik.eth" size={64} radius={10} />
<Avatar name="vitalik.eth" size={32} radius={0} />
<Avatar address="0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045" size={16} />
<Avatar address="0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045" size={8} />
```

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `address` | string | | Wallet address |
| `name` | string | | ENS name |
| `size` | number | `96` | Size in pixels |
| `radius` | number | fully rounded | Radius in pixels |

### Chain Icon

Access ConnectKit's `<ChainIcon>` component from within your app for consistency.

```tsx
import { ChainIcon } from "connectkit";
import { useAccount, useConfig } from "wagmi";

const { chain } = useAccount();
const { chains } = useConfig();

<ChainIcon id={1} />
<ChainIcon id={chain?.id} unsupported={chains.find((c) => c.id === chain?.id) === undefined} />
<ChainIcon id={chains?.[0].id} />
```

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | number | required | Chain ID |
| `unsupported` | boolean | | Show unsupported icon |
| `size` | number \| string | `24` | Width/height in px, or a percentage string |
| `radius` | number \| string | `50%` | Radius in px or percentage |

#### Supported chain icons

ConnectKit ships with icons for **583 chains**. The table on the docs page lists each Chain Name, Chain ID, and its icon (chains not supported by default show a small warning badge on their icon). Below is a representative sample of the table:

![Sample of the ConnectKit Chain Icon table showing chain names and their icons](data:image/png;base64,)

<details>

## SIWE Button

ConnectKit exports an easy-to-use Sign In With Ethereum button. Import `<SIWEButton>` and `useSIWE` to check if the user has successfully signed in.

### Here it is in action

![ConnectKit SIWE Button modal opened](data:image/png;base64,)

**Animation** — clicking the SIWE "Connect Wallet" button opens the modal. Recording included alongside this file:

![ConnectKit SIWE button demo animation](./connectkit-siwe-button-demo.gif)

```tsx
import { SIWEButton, useSIWE } from "connectkit";

// This component must be used within the SIWE context provider
const MyComponent = () => {
  const { isSignedIn, data } = useSIWE();
  return (
    <>
      <SIWEButton showSignOutButton />
      {isSignedIn && <p>Signed in as {data.address}</p>}
    </>
  );
};
```

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `showSignOutButton` | boolean | Displays a sign out button after signing in. |
| `onSignIn` | () => void | Event handler on a successful sign in. |
